creative_brief: Hero image: close-up of supermarket meat packaging — sterile, generic, no farm name visible — next to a FarmThru label showing a named NSW farm, practice, and short distance. Muted supermarket tones on left, warm natural light on right. Split-screen tension. Typography clean, sentence case throughout.
headline: Your supermarket knows exactly where your food comes from. They just won't tell you.
subhead: That silence isn't an accident — it's a business model. FarmThru is building a farm-direct grocery store where every product traces back to a named farm, a real practice, and a short supply chain you can actually verify.
hero_copy: Next time you're at the shops, flip over any packet of chicken. Look for the farm name. The feeding practice. The kilometres it travelled. You'll find a barcode, a use-by date, and a brand logo designed to feel trustworthy. What you won't find is a single answer. That's not an oversight. When food passes through four or five middlemen, nobody wants you asking questions. FarmThru works differently — farm to hub to you. We partner with roughly 15–20 farms and suppliers across NSW. Every product traces back to its source. No mystery. No middlemen hiding the story.

sections.heading: The less you know, the more they profit
sections.body: Supermarkets have spent decades training us to trust packaging instead of provenance. A green label. A cartoon farmer. The word 'natural' in a friendly font. None of it tells you what actually happened to your food before it hit the shelf. That opacity isn't laziness — it's strategy. When your beef travels 2,400km through processors, distributors, and warehouses, transparency becomes a liability. Every hand it passes through adds cost and removes accountability. FarmThru's model cuts that chain short. We work directly with partner farms across NSW. You order online, we source from the farm, and you collect from our Brookvale hub Monday to Friday. Two steps between paddock and pickup. That's it. When the supply chain is that short, there's nothing to hide.

sections.heading: What transparency actually looks like
sections.body: It's not a marketing slogan on a paper bag. It's a named farm on every product. It's knowing your eggs came from a specific property, not a warehouse code. It's regenerative practices you can read about before you buy — not after a food safety recall forces disclosure. Our hub-and-collect model keeps costs low so we can focus on what matters: real food from real farms, priced for everyday families. No subscription. No lock-in. No membership fees. You order when you want, collect from Brookvale when it suits you. Bulk buying options mean you save more when you commit more — rewarding the weekly shop, not punishing it.

sections.heading: We're building this in the open — and you can be part of it
sections.body: FarmThru is preparing an equity crowdfunding campaign through Birchal. That means everyday people — not just venture capitalists — can own a piece of a grocery store that believes you deserve to know what you're eating. A $5 refundable deposit secures your VIP spot: first access to the campaign, priority updates, and exclusive Q&A sessions before anyone else. Minimum investment starts at $50, with a cap of $10,000 per investor. This is early. There are real risks — like any business at this stage, success isn't guaranteed, and you could lose your investment. But if you believe the grocery system needs more transparency and less theatre, this is your chance to vote for that with your wallet.

sections.heading: Compliance
sections.body: FarmThru's equity crowdfunding campaign is being conducted through Birchal. This is not financial advice. Investment in early-stage businesses carries significant risk, including the potential loss of your entire investment. Past performance is not a reliable indicator of future results. You should consider whether this investment is appropriate for your financial situation and seek independent advice if needed. A $5 VIP deposit is fully refundable and does not constitute an investment or obligation to invest.
